prompt: A Hospital Operations Performance & Service-Area Management Console system with main features including offline KPI scoring, community/pickup-point configuration, housing bed-state management, analytics and exports, and locally hosted integration APIs for other hospital desktop modules. The application targets Windows 11 with an English UI, built with JavaFX for a responsive, keyboard-first desktop experience: operators can work in multiple parallel windows (e.g., “KPI Reviews,” “Pickup Points,” “Bed Board,” and “Reports”), use right-click context menus for common actions (assign leader, pause service, transfer bed, open audit trail), and rely on global shortcuts (Ctrl+F search, Ctrl+N new record, Ctrl+E export, Ctrl+L open logs). A system tray mode keeps long-running jobs (scheduled reports, archival, consistency checks) active while minimizing to tray, with a lock screen after 10 minutes of inactivity. Roles include System Administrator (configuration, access policies), Operations Manager (service-area and staffing decisions), Reviewer (peer/expert evaluations), Auditor (read-only compliance access), and Data Integrator (consumes local APIs). Performance evaluations support self/peer/expert scorecards with configurable metrics and weights that must total 100%; reviewers can recuse themselves when a conflict is flagged, appeals must be filed within 7 calendar days, and re-reviews require a second expert reviewer. Pickup-point management maintains address, hours, capacity caps, and temporary service pauses; matching is based on locally stored geozones and ZIP+street ranges, with manual override and an enforced “one active pickup point per community per day” rule. “Route tracking” is implemented as offline evidence intake: staff import checkpoint logs from handheld devices via USB; the console validates file format, masks precise coordinates to a 0.1-mile grid in standard views, and raises alerts for deviations beyond 0.5 miles or missed checkpoints beyond 15 minutes. All business logic runs locally in a closed loop, persisting to a local SQLite database with encrypted-at-rest sensitive fields (e.g., staff IDs, resident identifiers) and masked output for exports by default. The data model includes Community, ServiceArea, PickupPoint, LeaderAssignment, EvaluationCycle, Scorecard, Review, Appeal, BedBuilding/Room/Bed, and BedStateHistory, with state machines enforcing allowed transitions (e.g., bed can’t move from Occupied directly to OutOfService without a recorded checkout). The console exposes local REST APIs bound to 127.0.0.1 by default for querying/filtering/exporting results with pagination (default 50, max 500), sorting, field selection, and token-based rate limiting (60 requests/minute per local user); exports write to the file system as Excel/PDF/CSV with a required destination folder permission check and SHA-256 fingerprinting for tamper-evident archives. Logging and governance include tiered business/system logs, key-path tracing for critical workflows (evaluation approval, bed transfer, pickup-point pause), nightly local backups with 14-day retention, and monthly archival/cleanup of historical cycles older than 24 months; consistency validators detect orphaned records and state violations. Observability includes runtime health panels, exception alerts to an on-screen inbox, job scheduling for timeouts and rollback, crash-safe checkpoint resume for in-progress imports/exports, and offline package-based updates via signed installer (.msi) with one-click rollback to the previous version. The UI must support 1920x1080 and above with high-DPI scaling, and the app must remain stable in 8+ hour sessions through explicit resource release for files, threads, and database handles.
